title: Wingmen

jokes.text: where are you going with this, Bret?
jokes.setup: Bret continues his elaborate romantic song
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's matter-of-fact interruption mid-song
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan interruption of flowery nonsense. Jemaine's practical nature cutting through Bret's fantasy.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: where are you going with this, Bret?

jokes.text: when was this?
jokes.setup: Bret sings about days and years going by
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's literal-minded time check
jokes.explanation: Another perfect deadpan reality check. Jemaine treating the fantasy as reportable fact.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: when was this?

jokes.text: uh, Bret, could you please move over to your right?
jokes.punchline: Jemaine treating it as a TV viewing obstruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy - elaborate musical performance reduced to blocking the TV. Pure Flight of the Conchords absurdist mundanity.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: uh, Bret, could you please move over to your right?

jokes.text: oh, yeah? Sounds a bit gay.
jokes.setup: Bret sings about reminiscing about reminiscing
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's blunt, inappropriate assessment
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate insensitivity from Jemaine, though relies on casual homophobia for the punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: oh, yeah? Sounds a bit gay.

jokes.text: so did you... did you get the bread, or...
jokes.punchline: Jemaine returns to the original practical concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that deflates the entire musical number. Classic FOTC structure - elaborate fantasy meets mundane reality.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: so did you... did you get the bread, or...

jokes.text: ¶ she said her name was a secret ¶ then she said her name was cheri... ¶
jokes.setup: Jemaine asks what her name was
jokes.punchline: Logical contradiction delivered as profound mystery
jokes.explanation: Bret's romantic delusion creating nonsensical contradictions presented as poetry.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: she said her name was a secret then she said her name was cheri...

jokes.text: is her middle name cheri? So it's a secret cheri maybe?
jokes.punchline: Jemaine trying to logic through obvious nonsense
jokes.explanation: Jemaine's earnest attempt to make sense of Bret's fantasy creates absurd compound logic.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: is her middle name cheri? So it's a secret cheri maybe?

jokes.text: 'oh, frigid' - Jemaine's response to 'winter's morning'
jokes.setup: Bret sings she 'reminded me of a winter's morning'
jokes.punchline: Jemaine interprets this as sexually frigid
jokes.explanation: Another perfect deflation of romantic imagery with practical interpretation.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: oh, frigid

jokes.text: Bret, did any of this actually happen?
jokes.setup: Increasingly elaborate and impossible romantic narrative
jokes.punchline: Jemaine finally questions basic reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect structural moment - Jemaine's practical nature finally overwhelms his politeness. Good timing for reality check.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Bret, did any of this actually happen?

jokes.text: Bret reveals 95% was made up after elaborate fantasy
jokes.punchline: Almost entirely fabricated
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the extended setup, reveals character's fantasy-prone nature

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Bret reveals 95% was made up after elaborate fantasy

jokes.text: I made 95% of that up. Which bit's true? I saw a girl.
jokes.punchline: Reveals almost total fabrication from minimal reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of the entire opening. Quantifies the fantasy vs reality ratio perfectly. Great character moment for Bret's delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I made 95% of that up. Which bit's true? I saw a girl.

jokes.text: So your excuse for not getting the bread is because you were on a fantasy date?
jokes.setup: Learning the romantic encounter was 95% fictional
jokes.punchline: Jemaine summarizing the absurd excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reframes the absurdity. Jemaine's practical summary makes Bret's behavior even more ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: So your excuse for not getting the bread is because you were on a fantasy date?

jokes.text: Why don't you go on a real date? Because they're never as good as fantasy dates.
jokes.punchline: Bret's preference for fantasy over reality
jokes.explanation: Perfectly sums up Bret's character - preferring delusion to reality. Relatable yet absurd logic delivered with conviction.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Why don't you go on a real date?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Because they're never as good as fantasy dates.

jokes.text: She works down at the cheap zoo. The pet store?
jokes.punchline: Bret's pretentious renaming of pet store
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific euphemism. Shows Bret romanticizing mundane reality while Jemaine provides translation.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: She works down at the cheap zoo.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: The pet store?

jokes.text: She's, um, one of the cat tamers.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous job title for basic pet care
jokes.explanation: Continues Bret's grandiose reframing. 'Cat tamer' for pet store worker shows his romantic delusions extending to her profession.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: She's, um, one of the cat tamers.

jokes.text: Bret's goldfish buying compulsion and logic
jokes.setup: He freezes up and panics around the girl
jokes.punchline: He ends up buying goldfish he doesn't want because of a deal structure
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Bret's social anxiety and flawed logic. The 'buy two get one free' reasoning is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Well, if you get two, you get one free. But you don't even want any goldfish, Bret. Why would you buy two? Might as well get one free.
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about unwanted free fish
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic loop. Bret's anxiety manifesting as irrational shopping decisions with faulty economic reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, if you get two, you get one free. But you don't even want any goldfish, Bret. Why would you buy two? Might as well get one free.

jokes.text: Oh! When? Always. Oh, okay. So you do something every time, and suddenly you've got a reputation.
jokes.setup: Jemaine accused of always hitting on Bret's crushes
jokes.punchline: Bret's indignant response about pattern recognition
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict - Bret's defensive logic about behavioral patterns. Relatable relationship dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Oh! When?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Always.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Oh, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: So you do something every time, and suddenly you've got a reputation.

jokes.text: What are you doing in here? Fixing the shower. It wasn't broken. Well, I'm sorry... I broke it.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he broke it to fix it
jokes.explanation: Classic Murray incompetence - creating problems to solve them. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: What are you doing in here?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Fixing the shower.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: It wasn't broken.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Well, I'm sorry... I broke it.

jokes.text: Murray breaking then 'fixing' the shower
jokes.setup: Murray claims to be fixing the shower
jokes.punchline: Admits he broke it, then apologizes for breaking it
jokes.explanation: Typical Murray incompetence. The circular logic of breaking then apologizing for breaking is character-appropriate.

dialogue.line: Murray breaking then 'fixing' the shower

jokes.text: Hey! What's up, guys? Hey, Dave. What's with the fish, Bret? I've got a girlfriend.
jokes.punchline: Bret's delusional explanation for goldfish
jokes.explanation: Shows Bret's delusion extending to external explanations. Fish = girlfriend is absurd leap of logic.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Hey! What's up, guys?

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Hey, Dave. What's with the fish, Bret?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I've got a girlfriend.

jokes.text: Dave's confusion about their existence/New Zealand
jokes.punchline: Reveals he previously thought they were from 'never never land' and flew there
jokes.explanation: Establishes Dave's character as confused about basic reality. The New Zealand/Never Never Land confusion is mildly amusing.

dialogue.line: Dave's confusion about their existence/New Zealand

jokes.text: Guys, women like three things: Men in kilts, southern comfort, and Chris isaak's 'wicked game.' Whoa, he does know more than you.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and random list treated as universal truth
jokes.explanation: Dave's confident expertise on bizarre specifics. The randomness of 'Chris Isaak's Wicked Game' makes it memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Guys, women like three things: Men in kilts, southern comfort, and Chris isaak's 'wicked game.'
Whoa, he does know more than you.

jokes.text: Murray's elaborate band meeting protocol for three people
jokes.punchline: He goes through formal attendance protocol for just the three of them
jokes.explanation: Classic Murray bureaucratic absurdity. Taking formal meeting protocol seriously for a tiny group is perfectly in character.

dialogue.line: Murray's elaborate band meeting protocol for three people

jokes.text: uh, to be honest with you, at the end of the day, um... actually, I think greg could be responsible.
jokes.setup: Murray on phone about missing sandwiches
jokes.punchline: Immediately throws Greg under the bus
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate betrayal. Murray's cowardice and willingness to sacrifice Greg for convenience.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: uh, to be honest with you, at the end of the day, um... actually, I think greg could be responsible.

jokes.text: Well, it's like... it's kind of like a habit. You know, we sort of fall into these patterns. Day after day, we end up doing the same sort of thing.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he called pointless meeting
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describes the show's episodic structure
jokes.explanation: Subtle meta-comedy where character accidentally describes sitcom format. More clever than funny.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Well, it's like... it's kind of like a habit. You know, we sort of fall into these patterns. Day after day, we end up doing the same sort of thing.

jokes.text: Murray's relationship metaphor about old sneakers
jokes.setup: Murray explains how relationships become routine
jokes.punchline: Compares his marriage to old sneakers you take for granted
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor. The sneaker metaphor is somewhat tired but fits Murray's earnest personality.

dialogue.line: Murray's relationship metaphor about old sneakers

jokes.text: 'Chicken torso' as Bret's codename
jokes.setup: Jemaine tries to contact Bret via walkie-talkie
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Chicken torso' as a spy codename
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd codename. 'Chicken torso' is specific, weird, and completely unnecessary for the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: 'Chicken torso' as Bret's codename

jokes.text: Stand with your feet apart like your balls are on fire. Don't do the accompanying face, though... just the stance.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific visual metaphor with clarifying warning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist advice. The visual specificity and the warning about facial expression makes it exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Stand with your feet apart like your balls are on fire. Don't do the accompanying face, though... just the stance.

jokes.text: Half ignore her and half pay attention to her. Hello. Turn side on. Say somethin you assho! Compliment her hair. Ask her what's up with her face. What's up with your face?
jokes.punchline: Bret follows worst possible suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of bad advice leading to social disaster. Great cringe comedy setup and payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Half ignore her and half pay attention to her.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Hello.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Turn side on.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Say somethin you assho!

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Compliment her hair.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Ask her what's up with her face.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: What's up with your face?

jokes.text: Tell her you're freaky. Why would you tell her that he's freaky? How's he gonna get into her pants if he doesn't...
jokes.punchline: Murray's inappropriate sexual strategy
jokes.explanation: Murray's inappropriate sexual advice fitting his crude character. Sets up the 'freaky' song.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Tell her you're freaky.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Why would you tell her that he's freaky?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: How's he gonna get into her pants if he doesn't...

jokes.text: Mel's stalking advice including collecting nail clippings
jokes.setup: Mel gives advice about getting to know someone
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable advice to collecting nail clippings
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work and escalation. Mel's casual mention of nail clipping collection is perfectly disturbing and in character.

dialogue.line: Mel's stalking advice including collecting nail clippings

jokes.text: Going through Savannah's garbage and knowing specific bill amounts
jokes.setup: They're investigating the girl Bret likes
jokes.punchline: They've thoroughly searched her garbage and know intimate financial details
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the stalking behavior. The specific details make it funnier and more disturbing.

dialogue.line: Going through Savannah's garbage and knowing specific bill amounts

jokes.text: when you love someone, you need all the ammunition you can get to take them down.
jokes.punchline: Love as warfare metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark metaphor for relationships. Perfectly encapsulates Mel's predatory approach to romance.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: when you love someone, you need all the ammunition you can get to take them down.

jokes.text: Business stuff... you know, stock exchange, calculators.
jokes.setup: Telling Jemaine what to discuss with businessman
jokes.punchline: Reductive stereotypes of business topics
jokes.explanation: Bret's naive understanding of business conversation. 'Calculators' as small talk topic is particularly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Business stuff... you know, stock exchange, calculators.

jokes.text: I told you... did I not explain that? You know, this is bullshit. I didn't come out here to do pro Bono work.
jokes.setup: Learning they can't keep robbery proceeds
jokes.punchline: Professional mugger's workplace grievances
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy - treating mugging as legitimate profession with workplace standards. 'Pro bono' in crime context is inspired.

dialogue.speaker: John
dialogue.line: I told you... did I not explain that? You know, this is bullshit. I didn't come out here to do pro Bono work.

jokes.text: The mugging going wrong with John actually stealing the purse
jokes.punchline: John can't help himself and actually commits the crime
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the setup. John's inability to fake professionalism leads to actual crime, perfectly escalating the absurdity.

dialogue.line: The mugging going wrong with John actually stealing the purse

jokes.text: Murray's fake diplomatic immunity cards from Kinko's
jokes.setup: Murray tries to get them out of jail with diplomatic immunity
jokes.punchline: The cards are obviously fake and made at Kinko's for $10
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray logic. The casual admission about Kinko's and asking about the officer's cards shows his complete lack of awareness.

jokes.text: ¶ girl, I'm gonna take the month of august off ¶ just to get you off ¶
jokes.punchline: Sexual double meaning with dedication metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good opening for 'freaky' song. Double meaning works and sets up the absurdist escalation to follow.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: girl, I'm gonna take the month of august off just to get you off

jokes.text: 'I'm freaky' - Bret finally using the advice and launching into the song
jokes.setup: All episode Murray has been telling Bret to say he's freaky
jokes.punchline: Bret finally does it at the worst possible moment, leading to an elaborate song
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and setup for the musical number. The timing makes it both perfect and terrible advice.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I'm freaky

jokes.text: ¶ let's take my body and we'll cover it with honey ¶ stick some money to the honey ¶ now I'm covered in money, honey ¶
jokes.punchline: Bizarre honey-money scheme with wordplay
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist sexuality. The money-honey rhyme scheme with bizarre logistics makes it memorable and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: let's take my body and we'll cover it with honey

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: stick some money to the honey

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: now I'm covered in money, honey

jokes.text: How do you know about my electricity Bill? Well, I just went through your rubbish and I found your Bill.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of garbage stalking
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of creepy behavior. Bret's matter-of-fact delivery makes the stalking admission even more disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Savannah
dialogue.line: How do you know about my electricity Bill?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Well, I just went through your rubbish and I found your Bill.

jokes.text: She's a bit crazy. Do you want me to give you a hand breaking up with her?
jokes.punchline: Bret calling her crazy and Jemaine offering breakup help
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending irony - the stalker calling his victim crazy, with Jemaine's helpful offer for nonexistent relationship. Great character blindness.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: She's a bit crazy.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Do you want me to give you a hand breaking up with her?

tmdb_overview: Jemaine and Dave help Bret woo the clerk at the cheap zoo (pet shop). Jemaine also helps Murry seek forgiveness from Greg.
